campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies pay to operate in the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to these tax rates, and the council debated and voted on whether to approve the new rates for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases
Finance department proposal and presentation
Municipal code changes
Council deliberation and voting
Emergency preparedness office involvement

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could voice their opinions on a proposed new city rule (ordinance). After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by skipping the usual first reading requirement and planning to vote on adopting the ordinance at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for approval
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the town's finances for February. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign off on several construction and professional service contracts to move forward with various improvement projects around town.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and closed session records
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with multiple vendors including QLM, Inc.
Approved agreements for professional services (3-year contract mentioned)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for May 5th and May 12th, 2026. Council members reviewed what items should be added to each meeting, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked public records from mid-to-late April.
keyTopics: Planning the May 5 and May 12 council meeting agendas
Adding new items for upcoming council discussions
Setting specific times for agenda items to keep meetings organized
Reviewing public records from April 16-23, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes, hear from community members during public comment, and review funding requests for neighborhood grants in the 2026-27 budget year. The council also discussed attendance at neighborhood meetings and received updates from staff and committees.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comment period on community concerns
Council attendance and engagement at neighborhood meetings
Approval of previous meeting minutes

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on April 28, 2026 to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to watch and comment on the proceedings either in person at City Hall or online.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
In-person public comment opportunity
Online participation option for residents
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 30, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 30, 2026, to approve meeting notes from the Economic Development Committee's January and March sessions. The council also opened the floor for public comments on any city issues, though they reminded speakers that decisions generally can't be made on topics not formally listed on the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of January 29, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Approval of March 27, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Public comment period for community members
Administrative review of committee meeting records

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings near Levi's Stadium during the FIFA World Cup 2026 events. The council also approved a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters covering 2026-2029. One item was withdrawn from the agenda before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted around Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026
New 3-year contract agreement with Santa Clara firefighters
Staff withdrew one agenda item before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 where they discussed and approved several financial and development matters for the upcoming fiscal year. Key items included reviewing tax responsibility policies, approving the city's development budget for 2026-27, updating user fees and services, and discussing efforts to address homelessness in Santa Clara County.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 development budget approval
Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
User fees and service updates
Santa Clara County homelessness initiative
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss a proposal to subdivide a vacant 1-acre lot on Old Trace Road into 9 residential lots, which would allow for the construction of 9 new single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units (smaller secondary homes). The council also handled routine business including scheduling meetings and electing new leadership positions.
keyTopics: Proposal to build 9 new single-family homes on Old Trace Road
7 junior accessory dwelling units (small secondary homes) included in the project
Subdivision of 1-acre vacant lot into 9 separate lots
Project exempt from environmental review under state law
Election of new Council Chair and Vice Chair
